rules.title: AI is core to the product, not an auxiliary feature.
Total funding raised to date is below EUR 10M.
Company is a registered legal entity.
Team has at least two members or two dedicated cofounders.
Startup is pre-seed, seed, or Series A.
Live product or strong MVP exists.
Early traction or strong validation is packaged.
At least one founder can attend finals in Paris.
Dealum application payload and field map are ready.

integrations.name: External form draft and submission automation

dimensions.label: Team Strength
dimensions.gap: Add two named founder profiles, commitment, and Paris attendance.

dimensions.label: Product Distinctiveness
dimensions.gap: Show a live user-selected opportunity run in the video.

dimensions.label: Traction Or Validation
dimensions.gap: Collect five conversations, two written validations, and one paid or LOI signal.

dimensions.label: Opportunity Scale
dimensions.gap: Quantify reachable wedge: founders, accelerators, grants, hackathons, and RFPs.

dimensions.label: AI Depth
dimensions.evidence: V core, scorecard renderer, generated payloads, WAIBAv traces
dimensions.gap: Capture one env-backed model call and one live automation draft session.

competitors.name: Startup portals
competitors.strength: Distribution, profiles, program discovery, investor workflows.
competitors.weakness: They collect applications; they do not build the evidence package for founders.
competitors.counter: Complement Dealum/F6S by preparing the founder to submit stronger applications everywhere.

competitors.name: Grant and RFP platforms
competitors.strength: Clear ROI, proposal workflows, compliance matrices, buyer budget.
competitors.weakness: Often narrow to grants or enterprise proposals, not founder opportunity execution.
competitors.counter: Own the broader founder application-ops wedge: contests, accelerators, grants, RFPs, and MVP evidence.

competitors.name: Proposal writers
competitors.strength: Fast drafting and easy before/after demo.
competitors.weakness: Writing is only one artifact; judges need product, traction, media, forms, and receipts.
competitors.counter: Show the full lifecycle from source to payload to automation receipt.

competitors.name: Generic browser agents
competitors.strength: Broad automation narrative and impressive demos.
competitors.weakness: Weak domain memory, risky claims, and little judging/evidence structure.
competitors.counter: Use domain-specific scoring, private/public evidence boundaries, and final-submit gates.

competitors.name: Consultants and agencies
competitors.weakness: Manual, hard to scale, inconsistent receipts.
competitors.counter: Productize repeatable execution and leave experts for final judgment.

next_moves: Run a 72-hour proof sprint with 20 targets, 5 conversations, 2 validations, and 1 paid or LOI signal.
Record a judge-demo video that starts with the RAISE deadline and ends with Dealum payload readiness.
Generate one packet for a user-selected opportunity to prove this is not hand-authored for RAISE.
Publish redacted before/after time saved and missing-evidence detection metrics.
Add founder legal/team/funding secure inputs directly in Dealum before final submit.
